mnemonics: HADOOP Helps Analyze Data Under One Platform
keyPoints: Hadoop is used for storing and processing massive datasets across distributed clusters.
Common use cases include log analysis, recommendation systems, and fraud detection.
It provides a scalable and cost-effective solution for big data problems that traditional databases cannot handle.
commonMistakes: Confusing Hadoop (the framework) with its components like HDFS or MapReduce.
Assuming Hadoop is for real-time processing; it is primarily designed for batch processing.
Thinking Hadoop is a database; it is a file system and processing engine for unstructured/semi-structured data.
